methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Drones & Autonomous. Range: $5.0B - $7.6B.

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Joby Aviation, a public-stage drone and autonomous systems company, is analyzed against a universe of 6 private-sector peers in the Drones & Autonomous segment. The comparable set includes Archer Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, Elroy Air, and ideaForge, reflecting similar operational maturity and valuation tier.

The peer-median method is applicable and anchors the valuation analysis. Three directly comparable companies—Zipline (Series G, $7.6B), Nuro (Series G, $6.0B), and Archer Aviation (Public, $5.0B)—yield a median valuation of $6.0B, with a range spanning $5.0B to $7.6B. This method forms the sole triangulation point given available data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.1B to $6.9B, with a median estimate of $6.0B. Joby's current $10B+ valuation sits materially above peer comparables, indicating either market-forward pricing reflecting unquantified operational advantages, or premium multiples relative to the peer cohort. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 3-peer sample, stage-specific valuation adjustments between Series G private and public entities, and cash-burn profiles not reflected in static valuations. A single-method analysis limits confidence; inclusion of revenue multiple or cash-multiple frameworks would strengthen conviction.
